content.problemStatement:
You will be given two arrays AAA and BBB, each of length NNN. For some value KKK, let's call an array CCC `good` if it satisfies the following conditions:


0≤Ci≤Bi0\le C_i \le B_i0≤Ci​≤Bi​ for (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N)
**At least** KKK elements in the array must be equal to 000.
∑i=1NAi=∑i=1NCi\sum_{i=1}^N A_i = \sum_{i=1}^N C_i ∑i=1N​Ai​=∑i=1N​Ci​
Among all the `good` arrays CCC, let result(K)result(K)result(K) denote the **minimum** value of ∑i=1N∣Ai−Ci∣\sum_{i=1}^N |A_i-C_i|∑i=1N​∣Ai​−Ci​∣. If no good array is possible, result(K)=−1result(K) = -1result(K)=−1.

Find the value result(K)result(K)result(K) for all (0≤K<N)(0\le K \lt N)(0≤K<N).


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the array AAA.
The third line contains NNN integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, the array BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers, result(K)result(K)result(K) for (0≤K<N)(0 \le K \lt N)(0≤K<N).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 100100100.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

[10,1,4,1,5][10, 1, 4, 1, 5][10,1,4,1,5] is a good array with at least 000 zeroes and gives the minimum value for result(0)result(0)result(0).
[10,0,5,1,5][10, 0, 5, 1, 5][10,0,5,1,5] is a good array with at least 111 zeroes and gives the minimum value for result(1)result(1)result(1).
No good array is possible for the given constraints when K=2,3,K = 2, 3,K=2,3, or 444.

Test case 222:

[7,7,9][7, 7, 9][7,7,9] is a good array with at least 000 zeroes and gives the minimum value of result(0)result(0)result(0).
No good array is possible when K=1K = 1K=1 or 222.
